subject: PoC to improve performance of tosca models
commit_message:
PoC to improve performance of tosca models

As this is a PoC, it does not include any new or updated junit tests
(except to fix those broken by the changes).  This also means there is
cloned/similar code that could be eliminated with a bit of refactoring.

Tried a number of different things in an attempt to improve the
performance of policy-models.

With these changes, I saw the tosca models portion of the API REST calls
speed up by 2-3x, the query of a single policy type by 6x, and the query
of all policy types by 12x; everything was in the 100-200ms range (on my
laptop).

Made the following changes, all of which improved performance:

- Added validation flag to addFragment() to avoid validating when
  retrieving from the DB; this code was obviated by the next change:

- Created EntityTree class which is used to build a graph of all
  connections.  This is used to simplify and improve the performance of
  the getCascadedXxx() methods.  It also had the side effect of
  eliminating the validation code during retrieval (though it would be
  easy to add validation back into the tree with minimal performance
  impact).  Another side effect was that get-latest-version became a
  single dip in a HashMap

- Stored PdpSubgroups as a BLOB within PdpGroup, as PAP always needs the
  PdpGroup anyway

- Stored the various fields of ToscaServiceTemplate as BLOBs.  This
  was fairly straightforward, though had to remember to store the
  superclass "metadata" as a BLOB, too.  This netted a significant
  performance boost, but eventually modified the code further:

- Stored the various fields of ToscaServiceTemplate as a single BLOB.
  This resulted in an even more significant performance boost.  However,
  this proved to be a bit tricky: had to mark the fields @Transient to
  prevent JPA from putting them in the DB.  This meant that they had to
  be copied to/from the shared data structure when invoking merge().
  Unfortunately, the @PreUpdate and @PostLoad annotations only work when
  going to/from the DB, not to/from merge().  As a result, had to
  introduce a PfUpdateable interface so that the DefaultPfDao could do
  those steps manually

Issue-ID: POLICY-2874
Change-Id: I68e803fcfa14c0676b1ce190a291d8adec8ddd92
Signed-off-by: Jim Hahn <jrh3@att.com>
